title: NZD/USD weakens as US-Iran tensions spur risk aversion, support Dollar
body: NZD/USD trades around 0.5890 on Tuesday, down 0.35% on the day, after failing to hold above the 0.5900 level. The pair moves lower as the US Dollar (USD) strengthens, supported by increased demand for safe-haven assets.

title: EUR/USD rebounds as Fed decision nears and US-Iran tensions cap Dollar downside
body: The Euro (EUR) trims a part of its intraday losses against the US Dollar (USD) on Tuesday as the Greenback loses momentum, with traders repositioning ahead of the Federal Reserve’s (Fed) interest rate decision due on Wednesday, while continuing to monitor developments in the Middle East.

title: USD/JPY steadies after hawkish BoJ shift as Middle East tensions support Dollar
body: USD/JPY trades around 159.50 on Tuesday, up modestly by 0.07% on the day, after briefly falling below the 159.00 level following the Bank of Japan’s (BoJ) policy decision.

title: USD/CAD nudges higher before Fed-BoC double-header
body: USD/CAD climbs 0.33% on Tuesday, settling close to 1.3670 after a steady grind higher off Monday's intraday low near 1.3600.

title: Dow Jones Industrial Average futures wobble as Iran talks stall, Crude Oil jumps
body: The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) sees marginal gains on Tuesday's session, with futures holding near 49,200 and the underlying cash index adding roughly 0.1%, propped up by a 5% jump in Coca-Cola (KO) after a stronger-than-expected earnings print.

title: Gold drops to four-week low on firm dollar, Oil-driven inflation concerns
body: Gold (XAU/USD) slips to a near one-month low, pressured by a firmer US Dollar (USD) and mounting Oil-driven inflation concerns, as investors await clearer signals on stalled diplomatic efforts between the United States (US) and Iran to end the war.

title: USD/JPY: Yen rebound seen short-lived – MUFG
body: MUFG’s Lee Hardman argues that the recent Japanese Yen (JPY) rebound against the US Dollar (USD) is unlikely to last, as USD/JPY remains in a broader bearish Yen trend since the Middle East conflict began.

title: USD/CHF gains as US-Iran stalemate keeps Dollar supported, Fed in focus
body: USD/CHF edges higher on Tuesday as uncertainty around US-Iran efforts to end the war keeps risk sentiment fragile, supporting demand for the US Dollar (USD) while the Swiss Franc (CHF) struggles to gain despite its safe-haven appeal, amid Swiss National Bank (SNB) warnings of readiness to act agains

title: GBP/USD: Sentiment tempers fundamentally supported Pound – Scotiabank
body: Scotiabank strategists Shaun Osborne and Eric Theoret highlight that the Pound (GBP) is underperforming as political uncertainty around PM [Prime Minister] Starmer and fiscal policy weighs on sentiment, even as widening United Kingdom (UK) - United States (US) spreads support GBP fundamentals.

title: BOJ: Gradual tightening with higher inflation risks – UOB
body: United Overseas Bank’s Alvin Liew reports the Bank of Japan (BoJ) left its policy rate at 0.75% but signalled that the next move will be a hike as underlying inflation nears target and real rates stay very low.

title: Fed: Data-dependent path toward neutral – TD Securities
body: TD Securities strategists Oscar Munoz and Eli Nir argue that new inflation tools championed by incoming Fed Chair Kevin Warsh, including trimmed mean measures and a potential big-data price project, will not materially alter the policy outlook.

title: US CB Consumer Confidence edges higher to 92.8 in April
body: Consumer sentiment in the United States (US) improved slightly in April, with the Conference Board's (CB) Consumer Confidence Index rising to 92.8 in April from 92.2 in March.

title: GBP/USD Price Forecast: Bullish potential intact as traders await Fed/BoE policy meetings
body: The GBP/USD pair extends the previous day's retracement slide from the 1.3575 area, or over a one-week top, and attracts some follow-through selling on Tuesday.

title: Gold Price Forecast: XAU/USD looks to Fed verdict for next big break amid stalled US-Iran talks
body: Gold is trading listlessly near the $4,700 level in Asian trading on Tuesday, maintaining its familiar range, as focus now shifts toward the two-day Federal Reserve (Fed) monetary policy meeting starting later in the day.

title: Gold Price Forecast: XAU/USD under modest pressure ahead of central banks, war clarity
body: Spot Gold trades with a soft tone on Monday, still stuck to familiar levels. The precious metal changes hands in the $4,680 price zone in the American session, after peaking at around $4,730 earlier in the day.

title: USD/CAD Price Forecast: Bears retain control amid Iran jitters, ahead of BoC/Fed
body: The USD/CAD pair is seen extending its retracement from the 1.3715 resistance, or over a one-week set on Friday, and drifting lower for the second consecutive day.

title: AI could quickly become the Fed’s new problem
body: A strong economy and a flimsy labor market don’t typically go hand in hand. For the Fed and other central banks, the playbook has long been straightforward: weak hiring calls for lower interest rates, while an overheating economy and inflation above target justify tighter policy.

title: Gold Price Forecast: XAU/USD extends range play around $4,700, kicking off a Big week
body: Gold finds fresh demand to retake the $4,700 level in Asia on Monday, as the US Dollar pauses its advance amid a recovery in risk sentiment and ahead of the key Federal Reserve (Fed) monetary policy decision due later this week.

title: EUR/USD Weekly Forecast: War and central banks in the limelight
body: The EUR/USD pair ended a three-week winning streak and settled below the 1.1700 mark as the US Dollar (USD) strengthened amid Middle East uncertainty.

title: US Dollar Weekly: Will Kevin Warsh please President Trump?
body: United States President Donald Trump’s nominee to succeed Jerome Powell as Federal Reserve Chair, Kevin Warsh, faced senators, and there was no subtlety.

title: Fresh oil shock as UAE exits Saudi-controlled cartels
body: The United Arab Emirates (UAE) has said it is to leave the OPEC and OPEC+ cartels of oil-producing nations, potentially threatening greater turbulence for global energy markets ahead.

title: Man, 89, arrested after shootings leave several wounded in Athens
body: An 89-year-old man has been arrested after shootings in Athens left several people wounded.

title: Cartel leader tipped to succeed 'El Mencho' captured
body: Mexico's army has captured a cartel leader tipped to succeed notorious drug lord "El Mencho".

title: At least 14 dead in Indonesia train crash
body: At least 14 people have been killed in a train collision in western Indonesia, with another 84 injured, according to the country's train operator.

title: Man pleads guilty over terror plot to attack Taylor Swift concert
body: A man accused of plotting to attack Taylor Swift concerts in Vienna has pleaded guilty on the opening day of his trial.
